description: Citation-first deep research agent that identifies up to 20 software engineers using only publicly verifiable technical artefacts. No inferred skill, no titles-as-proxy, no uncited claims.
systemPrompt: You are a research-grade software engineering sourcing agent. Your sole task is to identify software engineers using ONLY publicly verifiable technical artefacts. You must obey the following rules at all times: 1. You may not make any claim without a citation. 2. Every factual statement must link to a source URL. 3. If evidence is missing, ambiguous, or contradictory, exclude the profile. 4. You must not infer skill, seniority, or impact from job titles. 5. You must inspect actual artefacts: code, writing, talks, discussions. 6. You must return NO MORE THAN 20 profiles. 7. It is acceptable to return fewer than 20 profiles if validation fails. 8. Your output must be a structured research report, not a list. 9. You must include a methodology and limitations section. 10. You must never generate outreach, rankings, or opinions. If you cannot verify a claim, you must say nothing. Silence is preferable to speculation.

userStories.title: Project scaffold and gating question system
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need the project structure and mandatory entry gating so the agent refuses to run without required inputs (engineering focus, languages, depth expectation, evidence priorities).
acceptanceCriteria: Project has TypeScript entry point that executes
Gating prompts all five mandatory questions before proceeding
Agent refuses execution if any gating answer missing
Gating answers persisted to config JSON file
No default values assumed for any gating field
Config schema validates required fields exist and non-empty
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
Unit test confirms gating blocks on missing input
userStories.notes: Gating questions: 1) Target engineering focus, 2) Required languages (max 3), 3) Minimum depth expectation, 4) Evidence priority (choose 2-3 from: GitHub repos, OSS contributions, tech blogs, conference talks, SO depth), 5) Geography constraints (optional). The 'optional' geography still must be explicitly asked.

userStories.title: Approved data source registry with enforcement
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need a source registry that defines which data sources are approved, secondary, and explicitly disallowed, with runtime enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: Source registry config defines primary and secondary tiers
Disallowed sources list blocks LinkedIn scraping and paid DBs
Source validation function rejects disallowed URL patterns
Primary sources include GitHub repos and commit history
Primary sources include personal and technical blogs
Primary sources include conference talks on YouTube
Secondary sources include Stack Overflow profiles only
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Primary (Open Web Only): GitHub repos/commits, personal/Medium blogs, conference talks (YouTube, event pages), RFCs/whitepapers, public GitHub discussions/PRs. Secondary (Read-Only): Stack Overflow profiles/answers, GitHub profile summaries. Disallowed: LinkedIn scraping, paid databases, resume aggregators, skill inference tools, 'About me' claims without artefacts.

userStories.title: Candidate discovery engine via public artefacts
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to discover an initial pool of candidate engineers by searching public technical artefacts matching the gating criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery searches GitHub repos matching target languages
Discovery searches technical blogs matching depth focus
Discovery searches conference talk listings for speakers
All search queries derived from gating config values
Each discovered candidate has at least one source URL
Discovery respects approved source registry constraints
Candidates stored with raw evidence links attached
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Discovery is the WIDE net phase. It finds candidates to evaluate, not validated profiles. Use WebSearch and WebFetch for public artefact discovery. Store raw results — validation happens in US-004.

userStories.title: Citation-bound validation framework with five signals
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to validate each discovered candidate against five binary signals, where each signal requires at least one citation URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Five signals defined: Code, Depth, Sustained, Thinking, Peer
Each signal evaluation returns binary present or absent
Every present signal includes minimum one citation URL
Profile included only when three or more signals present
Profiles with fewer than three signals are discarded
No signal uses job title as evidence input
Validation results stored per candidate with citations
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Signals: Code Signal (non-trivial repos, meaningful PRs), Depth Signal (systems/infra/performance/scaling concerns), Sustained Activity (evidence across 12+ months), Thinking Signal (blogs, RFCs, long-form explanations), Peer Recognition (accepted PRs, referenced work, high-signal SO answers). Signals are binary — no weighting, no scoring.

userStories.title: Hallucination control enforcement layer
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need hard enforcement rules that prevent any factual statement without a URL, any inferred seniority, and any paraphrased code intent without inspection.
acceptanceCriteria: Every factual claim in output has a URL citation
No statement infers seniority from years alone
No statement summarises skills without linked artefacts
Contradictory sources cause automatic profile exclusion
Enforcement runs as post-processing validation pass
Violations flagged with specific offending statement shown
Zero uncited factual claims in final report output
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Hard rules from section 8: No factual statement without a URL. No paraphrasing code intent without inspection. No inferred seniority. No summarised skills without artefacts. Contradictory sources → discard profile. This is the most critical quality gate.

userStories.title: Structured research report generator
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to generate a structured markdown report with metadata, methodology, validated profiles (max 20), sources, and limitations.
acceptanceCriteria: Report includes metadata: agent name and execution date
Report includes methodology section with validation rules
Each profile has summary, verified evidence with links, synthesis
Report caps at twenty validated profiles maximum
Report includes complete source URL list at end
Report includes research limitations section with gaps
Report outputs as structured markdown file on disk
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Output format from section 7. Metadata: agent name, execution date, role focus, languages, evidence priorities, profiles reviewed, profiles returned. Each profile: Name/handle, primary languages, engineering focus, geography, verified evidence (each bullet = direct link), synthesis from cited facts only. Must include full sources list and research limitations. CRITICAL: Report header must state 'This is research input requiring human review before any outreach. This is not an evaluation or ranking.' Human-in-the-loop is mandatory — the agent produces research, never sends outreach.

userStories.title: Quality hardening: strictness, relevance, and clarity
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need post-generation quality passes that enforce evidence strictness, signal quality depth, language/scope alignment, and report clarity.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence strictness pass removes all uncited sentences
Signal quality pass discards shallow activity profiles
Language alignment pass verifies target language relevance
Report clarity pass ensures audit-ready readability
Each pass logs what it changed or removed
Quality passes run automatically after report generation
Final output passes all four quality checks cleanly
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Maps to original iterations 1-4: (1) Evidence Strictness — eliminate uncited claims, (2) Signal Quality — confirm depth not surface activity, (3) Language & Scope Alignment — verify target language relevance, (4) Report Clarity — audit-ready rewrite without adding facts. These run as automated post-processing, not interactive loops.

userStories.title: End-to-end integration test with sample parameters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an end-to-end test that runs the full agent pipeline with sample gating inputs and verifies the complete output meets all quality standards.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test provides sample gating inputs automatically
Test executes full pipeline: gate, discover, validate, report
Output report passes hallucination control checks at zero violations
All quality hardening passes complete without manual intervention
Test verifies report structure matches required output format
Test confirms fewer than twenty-one profiles in output
Test runs and completes without authentication or paid APIs
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: This is the final stress test (maps to original iteration 5 — Hallucination Stress Test). Use sample inputs like: focus=Backend, languages=[Go, Rust], depth=distributed systems, evidence=[GitHub repos, Technical blogs]. The test should be repeatable and deterministic in structure (though web results may vary). Confirms the whole system works end-to-end before human review.

userStories.title: Iterative feedback loop with candidate exclusion
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need the agent to accept calibrated feedback after each run and produce a fresh batch of up to 20 different engineers on the next run, never repeating previously returned candidates unless explicitly requested.
acceptanceCriteria: Exclusion list persists previously returned candidate identifiers
Next run automatically excludes all prior returned candidates
Feedback input accepts free-text calibration notes from user
Feedback like 'show me 20 more' triggers fresh discovery
Calibration adjusts search parameters based on feedback content
Exclusion list file is human-readable JSON on disk
Agent confirms exclusion count before starting new discovery
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: This is the iterative refinement system. After each run, the agent writes all returned candidate identifiers (name/handle + primary URL) to an exclusion list (e.g., exclusions.json). On next run, the discovery engine filters OUT all excluded candidates before validation. Feedback types: (1) 'show me 20 more' — same criteria, fresh candidates, (2) calibrated feedback like 'more systems-focused' or 'less frontend' — adjusts gating parameters while maintaining exclusions, (3) 'include [name] again' — explicit override to remove from exclusion list. The exclusion list grows across runs, ensuring the agent never resurfaces the same high-scoring candidates. This makes the agent a true iterative research tool, not a one-shot ranker.

userStories.title: Supabase persistence layer for validated profiles
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need all validated engineer profiles pushed to a Supabase database after each run, creating a growing knowledge base the agent can reference in future searches.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase client connects using env variable credentials only
Validated profiles upserted with all citation URLs preserved
Each profile stores signals, languages, focus, and geography
Each profile stores last_validated timestamp for staleness tracking
Exclusion list syncs bidirectionally with database records
Run metadata stored: search parameters, date, profiles returned
Database write happens after report generation completes successfully
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: Supabase schema: Table 'engineers' — id (uuid), name (text), handle (text), primary_url (text, unique), languages (text[]), engineering_focus (text), geography (text), signals (jsonb — {code: bool, depth: bool, sustained: bool, thinking: bool, peer: bool}), citations (jsonb — array of {signal: string, url: string, description: string}), last_validated (timestamptz), created_at (timestamptz), updated_at (timestamptz). Table 'search_runs' — id (uuid), config (jsonb — the gating answers), profiles_discovered (int), profiles_validated (int), profiles_returned (int), exclusion_count (int), created_at (timestamptz). Table 'exclusions' — id (uuid), engineer_id (uuid FK), search_context (text — which search excluded them), excluded_at (timestamptz). Credentials via SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in .env file. NEVER commit .env. Agent should gracefully degrade if Supabase is unavailable (fall back to local-only mode).

userStories.title: Database-first discovery with web fallback
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need the agent to query its Supabase database BEFORE searching the open web, using cached validated profiles to reduce token usage and speed up results.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent queries Supabase first matching current search criteria
DB profiles older than ninety days flagged for re-validation
Fresh DB matches included in results without web re-discovery
Web discovery only runs to fill gap to twenty profiles
Agent reports how many profiles came from DB versus web
Dead citation URLs detected and flagged during DB retrieval
Stale profiles re-validated via web before inclusion in report
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: This is the key efficiency gain. Three-tier lookup: (1) Query Supabase for validated profiles matching current gating criteria (language, focus, geography). (2) Filter out excluded candidates and stale profiles (last_validated > 90 days ago). (3) If DB returns N valid profiles where N < 20, run web discovery for (20 - N) more candidates only. Over time, the DB becomes the primary source and web becomes the fallback. Example: Run 1 finds 18 via web, stores all 18 in DB. Run 2 (same criteria, different exclusions) — DB has 18, 15 still valid after exclusions, web only needs to find 5 more. Run 10 — DB has 150+ engineers, most runs barely touch the web. Report metadata should show 'Sources: 12 from database, 8 from web discovery' so the user can see the efficiency gain.

userStories.title: PAI skill wrapper for permanent invocation
userStories.description: As a user, I need the agent registered as a PAI skill so I can invoke it by saying 'find engineers', 'source developers', or 'codesignal' without navigating to the project directory or remembering commands.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md created at ~/.claude/skills/CodeSignal20/SKILL.md
Skill triggers on 'find engineers' and 'source developers'
Skill triggers on 'codesignal' and 'talent search'
Run workflow invokes agent from project directory automatically
New search workflow resets config but preserves exclusion history
Resume workflow continues existing search with feedback input
Status workflow shows current exclusion count and past runs
Typecheck passes with zero errors on project
userStories.notes: This is what makes the agent PERMANENT. After Ralph builds the code (US-001 through US-011), this story wraps it as a PAI skill at ~/.claude/skills/CodeSignal20/ with SKILL.md and Workflows/ (Run.md, NewSearch.md, Resume.md, Status.md). The skill points to /Users/robertpeacock/Projects/codesignal-20/ as the execution directory. Three distinct workflows: (1) Run/NewSearch — fresh gating questions, clean config, starts from scratch but does NOT reset exclusions from other searches, (2) Resume — takes feedback ('show me 20 more', calibration notes), maintains exclusion list, runs next batch, (3) Status — shows how many candidates have been found, excluded, how many runs completed, and Supabase DB stats (total engineers stored, breakdown by language/focus). This means RPBW can just say 'find me Go engineers' or 'source backend developers' from ANY Claude Code session and the agent runs.
